digest.component_or_area: index-lock retry logic in git store
propositions: The contended-lock tests use a threading.Timer(0.4, unlink) that can release the lock before git's first attempt, so on slow runners the retry path _with_index_lock_retry is never exercised.

A module-level _retry_probe test seam (default None, guarded) should be added to _with_index_lock_retry, fired after the initial run_once and after each in-loop run_once with a monotonic attempt_number.

The contended-lock tests must be rewritten to release the planted lock only after the confirmed first index.lock failure via the probe, asserting at least two attempts occurred and attempt 1 was a genuine index.lock error.
Each test file needs a dedicated test driving a non-index.lock git failure and asserting the probe observed exactly one attempt (non-lock failures are never retried).
The seam must not affect production since the default None guards the invocation, and callers importing the function by name see no behavior change.
The final snapshot/tombstone event must still exist and make test must pass green.
